extracted_text:
Module 10: Streamlit Dashboard

What dashboard does: choose a movie
see recommendations
see similarity results
see charts about taste
understand why recommendations were given.

Possible Dashboard Sections: search bar for movie
user profile panel
recommended movie cards
explanation text
chart for genres, decades, actors
sentiment score view

Skills: Streamlit basics, displaying tables/cards/charts, calling backend/model

Module 11: Evaluation & Testing

What can be evaluated? 
recommendations relevant?
do users get movies they may like?
does hybrid perform better than single methods?
does sentiment re-ranking help?

Simple Metrics: RMSE
Precision
Recall

matched_points: Dashboard explains why recommendations were made
Dashboard should mention backend or model integration
Evaluation should test relevance of recommendations
Evaluation should test whether users get movies they may like
Testing should mention usefulness of similarity results
missing_points: Hybrid system should be compared against individual methods

matched_points: Metrics should explain what RMSE measures
Metrics should explain what Precision measures
Metrics should explain what Recall measures

extracted_text:
ANSWER SHEET
=====================================
Student Name : Alex Johnson
Student ID : STU001
Exam : Introduction to Computer Science
Date : 18 May 2026
=====================================

Question 1 (10 marks)
What is an Operating System? Give examples.
An operating system is system software that manages the hardware and software resources of a computer. It provides an interface between the user and the computer hardware. Common examples of operating systems are Windows, Linux, and macOS.

Question 2 (10 marks)
Define RAM. Explain its role and key characteristics.
RAM is Random Access Memory. It is used by the computer to store data and programs that are currently in use. RAM is temporary storage · the data is lost when the computer is switched off.

Question 3 (10 marks)
Describe the role of the CPU in a computer system.
[No answer provided]

matched_points: An operating system manages hardware and software resources
It provides a user interface and services for application programs
Examples include Windows, Linux, and macOS
RAM stands for Random Access Memory
It is volatile — data is lost when power is removed
RAM stores currently running programs and active data
missing_points: More RAM allows more programs to run simultaneously
The CPU (Central Processing Unit) fetches, decodes, and executes instructions
It contains the ALU (Arithmetic Logic Unit) for calculations
The Control Unit coordinates all CPU operations
Clock speed in GHz measures how many cycles per second the CPU performs

overall_reasoning: Moderate performance with some missing points.

ANSWER SHEET
=====================================
Student Name : Alex Johnson
Student ID : STU001
Exam : Introduction to Computer Science
Date : 18 May 2026
=====================================

Question 1 (10 marks)
What is an Operating System? Give examples.
An operating system is system software that manages the hardware and software resources of a computer. It provides an interface between the user and the computer hardware. Common examples of operating systems are Windows, Linux, and macOS.

Question 2 (10 marks)
Define RAM. Explain its role and key characteristics.
RAM is Random Access Memory. It is used by the computer to store data and programs that are currently in use. RAM is temporary storage · the data is lost when the computer is switched off.

Question 3 (10 marks)
Describe the role of the CPU in a computer system.
[No answer provided]

matched_points: An operating system manages hardware and software resources
It provides a user interface and services for application programs
Examples include Windows, Linux, and macOS
RAM stands for Random Access Memory
It is volatile — data is lost when power is removed
RAM stores currently running programs and active data
missing_points: More RAM allows more programs to run simultaneously
The CPU (Central Processing Unit) fetches, decodes, and executes instructions
It contains the ALU (Arithmetic Logic Unit) for calculations
The Control Unit coordinates all CPU operations
Clock speed in GHz measures how many cycles per second the CPU performs

overall_reasoning: Moderate performance with some missing points.
